title: selaginella rupestris var. acanthonota
intro: Selaginella rupestris var. acanthonota is a rare, terrestrial species of spore-producing plant, commonly known as a resurrection fern due to its ability to revive itself after drying out, belonging to the family Selaginellaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Selaginella rupestris var. acanthonota include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
